decisions.summary: Handoff is a third fork mode, not a parallel subsystem. Refactor SummaryForkOptions.plain: boolean → forkMode: 'summary' | 'plain' | 'handoff' and reuse the existing fork pipeline (session reservation, spawn, issue link, dashboard fork dialog).

The handoff-authoring prompt is an inlined orchestrator-injected template at roles/handoff.md, modeled after the harness-agnostic review sub-role prompts. It is NOT a discoverable agent skill in .claude/agents/ or .claude/skills/.

Handoff doc path: ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/handoffs/<source-conv-id>-<iso-ts>.md, with a <source-conv-id>-<iso-ts>.md.done sentinel sidecar written by the agent on completion. Timestamp makes paths collision-free; sentinel separation lets the orchestrator distinguish 'doc started' from 'doc finished'.

Live-agent handshake uses the existing deliverAgentMessage() primitive (channels with tmux fallback). No new tmux semantics, no new CLI 'pan handoff-ready' signal — the .done sentinel is the only completion marker.

Handshake timeout defaults to 5 minutes (300_000ms). On timeout, on source-ended, or on doc-validation failure, the pipeline auto-falls back to summary fork — the command always produces a working new conversation.

Doc validation rules: non-empty (≥200 chars after trim) AND contains an H2 'Suggested skills' section (case-insensitive match for '## Suggested skills' or '## Suggested Skills'). Redaction of secrets is instructed in the prompt but NOT enforced by the validator (too brittle, would produce false positives on legitimate config snippets).

Endpoint shape: extend the existing POST /api/conversations/:name/summary-fork to accept a forkMode field in the body, with legacy plain: true mapping to forkMode: 'plain' for backwards compatibility. No new route.

Dashboard ForkModal exposes the handoff mode as a fourth mode in the existing checkbox/radio layout, with a 'Focus' text input that maps to the --focus CLI argument. The Fork button is disabled when handoff mode is selected and the source conversation is not running.

Handoff artifact: store handoff doc path on the target conversation row (new conversations column handoff_doc_path) AND on the source via a back-reference (handoff_target_conv_id). Add GET /api/conversations/:name/handoff-doc to read the doc text for the dashboard viewer.

Per SKILLS-CONVENTION, ship /pan-handoff as a wrapper skill at skills/pan-handoff/SKILL.md (also under sync-sources/skills/) with frontmatter description 'pan handoff <conv> — agent-authored conversation handoff that spawns a new conversation'. Listed in the linter's wrappers, not in the exclusion list.

hazards.summary: deliverAgentMessage is fire-and-forget under channels and best-effort under tmux. There is no acknowledgement signal indicating the source agent actually received and acted on the handoff prompt.
hazards.mitigation: Treat doc + sentinel observation as the only ground truth for handshake completion. Poll the filesystem for both files; if either is missing at timeout, fall back to summary fork. Do not try to detect 'agent saw the message' through tmux capture-pane — too brittle.

hazards.summary: Source agent may be mid-tool-call when the handoff prompt arrives, queuing the prompt behind in-flight work. The 5-minute timeout may not be enough for an agent that is busy in long-running tool use (e.g. a multi-minute build).
hazards.mitigation: Document the timeout in the prompt and CLI help; expose --timeout-ms as a future option (NOT in v1). Fallback to summary fork is the safety net.

hazards.summary: Source agent may write the doc but not the sentinel (or vice-versa) if it crashes mid-write.
hazards.mitigation: Require BOTH files exist before reading the doc. Treat single-file presence as 'still in progress' until timeout, then fail to summary fork.

hazards.summary: Workspace devcontainers do NOT mount ~/.panopticon (single-deacon invariant in .claude/rules/single-deacon-invariant.md). A conversation running inside a workspace devcontainer cannot write to ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/handoffs/ from the host's POV.
hazards.mitigation: In v1, the pre-handshake precondition check refuses handoff mode for conversations running inside workspace devcontainers and auto-falls back to summary fork with a recorded reason. Detect this by checking conv.cwd is a workspace path (starts with workspaces/) AND a workspace docker stack is up. Defer in-container handoff support to a follow-up issue.

hazards.summary: The handoff prompt itself, if injected at a bad moment, could leak into the conversation transcript and confuse the source agent's normal flow once the handoff completes.
hazards.mitigation: Make the prompt explicit and self-contained: state that this is a one-shot handoff request, give exact output path and sentinel filename, and instruct the agent to resume normal work after writing both files. Document this expectation in roles/handoff.md.

hazards.summary: Pi runtime path: Pi cannot consume Claude JSONL, and Pi conversations have different transcript semantics. The existing plain-fork pipeline already rejects Pi launch. Handoff seeds a plain-text message (the doc), so it behaves like summary fork — Pi launch should be fine.
hazards.mitigation: Treat handoff mode the same as summary mode for harness allow-list purposes. Pi source conversations: allow handoff (the source authors the doc; doc is portable text). Pi target conversations: allow handoff launch (the doc is injected via the Pi FIFO post-spawn, same as summary).

hazards.summary: Concurrent handoff requests on the same source conversation could race on the doc/sentinel paths.
hazards.mitigation: The path includes a millisecond-precision timestamp, so collisions are practically impossible. No additional locking required; document this in handoff-paths.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1358 (auto mode)
Auto-mode planning complete: 13 beads, 3 with requiresInspection (2 deep, 1 fast). Decisions recorded; no contradictions surfaced.
